raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 253 introduces legislation to streamline the review and issuance of post-entitlement permits needed to build new homes in California.",
    "The bill allows developers and homeowners to enlist licensed third-party professionals to expedite the permitting process if local building departments take longer than 30 days to conduct reviews.",
    "Streamlining post-entitlement permitting is crucial to addressing bottlenecks at the local level and turning approved projects into homes.",
    "Delays in local government approvals for housing developments are a significant constraint on housing production in California.",
    "On average, fully-entitled apartment buildings in California take approximately nine months to secure post-entitlement permits.",
    "Delays in obtaining post-entitlement permits can discourage homeowners from constructing smaller housing projects and may lead to project abandonment.",
    "AB 253 aims to cut bureaucratic red tape and streamline post-entitlement plan checks for certain residential projects.",
    "The bill provides an alternative mechanism for applicants to use licensed third-party professionals in the permitting stage, following best practices from countries like the United Kingdom and Japan."
  ]
}
```
